title: Debtor-party-resolved record (v5.4.0 pipeline stage 2 output)
description: The output of the debtor party engine (knowledge_base/architecture/17_debtor_party_rules.md). It is a raw event record (raw_event_record.schema.json) with the 17-derived debtor identity attached: owner_name, owner_type, filer_entity, and the resolution verdict. When 17.E routing fires (expected debtor missing, a known filer matches the proposed owner, a document-body debtor is not extractable, or no 17.C rule exists for the doc type), debtor_resolution_status is REVIEW_REQUIRED, owner_name is the 17.E placeholder, and review_reason is populated. v5.4.0 finding F-1 — RATIFIED Session 2 (see 17.K): the 17 engine records its verdict ONLY in debtor_resolution_status and MUST NOT write parcel_resolution_status or any other parcel-stage field; parcel_resolution_status first appears on the leads_base record. v5.4.0 Session 7A (see 17.K) adds the multi-owner block (owners, primary_owner_name, additional_owner_names, owner_count, multi_owner_status); owner_name stays the required primary display owner and old single-owner records remain valid.

raw_event_id.description: Back-reference to the raw_event_record this was resolved from.

owner_name.description: The resolved debtor / lead subject per 17.C — the PRIMARY display owner. When debtor_resolution_status is REVIEW_REQUIRED this is the 17.E placeholder '<canonical_doc_type> against unidentified party' — never a filer name. Backward-compatible: required, unchanged by Session 7A.

owner_type.description: Owner-type classification per 17.F. Precedence ENTITY > ESTATE > TRUST > INDIVIDUAL; UNKNOWN only when the name is empty or pure punctuation.

filer_entity.description: The original filer / lienholder / claimant name from the raw record (17.E). Populated whenever a filer is identifiable — including on RESOLVED records. Null when no filer could be identified, e.g. the F-5 default rule (no 17.C rule for the doc type) routes before any filer determination. A known-filer pattern may appear here and ONLY here — never in owner_name (20_semantic_verification_contract.md Check 12).

debtor_resolution_status.description: The debtor party engine's sole verdict field. RESOLVED = a real debtor was extracted per 17.C. REVIEW_REQUIRED = 17.E routing fired (expected debtor missing, a known filer matched the proposed owner, a document-body debtor was not extractable, or no 17.C rule exists for the doc type — the F-5 default). v5.4.0 finding F-1, RATIFIED Session 2 (see 17.K): the 17 engine does NOT write parcel_resolution_status; the REVIEW_REQUIRED verdict is propagated to parcel_resolution_status later, by the leads_base writer. Session 7A: this remains the single needs-review verdict field — multi_owner_status is descriptive only and never carries a review verdict.

review_reason.description: The 17.E rule that triggered REVIEW_REQUIRED routing — e.g. 'expected_debtor_name_type TP missing' or 'known_filer_pattern match on STATE OF <*>'. Null when debtor_resolution_status is RESOLVED.

expected_debtor_name_type.description: The name_type the 17.C rule designated as the debtor source for this canonical_doc_type. Audit field; null for document-body extraction.

debtor_extraction_method.description: How owner_name was derived: from the 17.C expected name_type, from the fallback name_type, extracted from document_body_text, or REVIEW_ROUTED when 17.E fired.

owners.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A multi-owner block — every distressed owner the 17 engine identified on the source document. Co-owners are NEVER dropped: every identified owner appears here regardless of multi_owner_status. A single-owner record carries exactly one entry, is_primary true. Optional for backward compatibility; old single-owner records that omit the multi-owner block remain valid.

primary_owner_name.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — the primary display owner; equals owner_name when multi_owner_status is SINGLE_OWNER or MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR; the 17.E placeholder when MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR (ownership priority is never guessed).

additional_owner_names.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — names of every non-primary owner. Empty for SINGLE_OWNER.

owner_count.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — count of entries in owners.

multi_owner_status.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — DESCRIPTIVE owner-cardinality status, NOT a review verdict. It describes owner count and primary clarity only. The needs-review verdict stays debtor_resolution_status (the F-1-ratified field). multi_owner_status has no REVIEW_REQUIRED value by design; when the primary owner is unclear it is MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR and debtor_resolution_status is independently REVIEW_REQUIRED — the descriptive and verdict fields answer different questions and can never contradict.

allOf.description: 17.E / v5.4.0 F-1: a REVIEW_REQUIRED debtor verdict carries a non-empty review_reason and REVIEW_ROUTED extraction. filer_entity is populated when a filer was identified and is null when routing fired before any filer determination (the F-5 default rule). The 17 engine writes no parcel-stage field.
A RESOLVED debtor verdict carries no review_reason and is not REVIEW_ROUTED.
v5.4.0 Session 7A — SINGLE_OWNER: exactly one owner, marked primary; owner_count 1; no additional owners.
v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR: more than one owner, exactly one marked primary; owner_count > 1.

v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR: more than one owner, NONE marked primary; owner_count > 1. multi_owner_status only describes; the verdict is separate — debtor_resolution_status MUST independently be REVIEW_REQUIRED, so the descriptive and verdict fields never contradict.
